elements.text: Here, we see that the search results are structured as links whose parent element is a <h 3> tag with class "".

elements.id: docling_book_en_搬书匠-3282-Python Web Scraping 2nd Edition-2017-英文版_page_195_23
elements.text: To scrape the search results, we will use a CSS selector, which was introduced in Chapter 2, Scraping the Data:

elements.id: docling_book_en_搬书匠-3282-Python Web Scraping 2nd Edition-2017-英文版_page_195_24
elements.text: >>> from 1xml.html import fromstring >>> import requests ››> html = requests get ('https: //www.google.com/search?q=test') >>> tree = fromstring (html.content) >>> results = tree.cssselect ('h3.r a') >>> results [<Element a at 0x7f3d9affeaf8>, ‹ Element a at 0x7f3d9affe890>, ‹ Element a at 0x7f3d9affe8e8>, ‹Element a at 0x7f3d9affeaal>, ‹ Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a968>, ‹ Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9c58>, ‹ Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9ec0>, ‹ Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9f18>, ‹ Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9£70>, ‹Element a at 0x7f3d9b1a9fc8>]
